mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market expansion and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 500,000 units signals a severe contraction in residential construction and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of cases when HOUST falls below 500.0, or the threshold fails to separate bull and bear market outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and decelerating real GDP growth creates a restrictive financial environment that suppresses corporate profitability and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and low GDPC1 growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales reflect immediate consumer demand strength, boosting short-term market sentiment and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive power when consumer debt-to-income ratios exceed historical averages.
